mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cut periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.1) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods when industrial production increases, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in > 65% of high-inflation months, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust job creation and labor market tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll-unemployment signal yields negative returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real gross domestic product indicate economic contraction and reduced corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP contractions are followed by positive equity returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.01) across business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above its short-term moving average signals accelerating manufacturing momentum and improved corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Breakout signals are followed by negative returns in > 55% of cases, or the moving average crossover strategy underperforms random walk benchmarks.
